decisions.question: What is the on-disk format and location of the snapshot provenance marker?
How does niwa atomically swap the snapshot directory while preserving instance.json?
decisions.chosen: Two-rename swap on sibling staging dir; instance.json relocated to <workspace>/.niwa-state/instance.json with dual-path fallback for legacy <workspace>/.niwa/instance.json (lazy migration on first SaveState, fallback removal scheduled v1.1)

decisions.question: Where does the canonical slug parser live and what does its API surface look like?
decisions.chosen: New leaf package internal/source/ exposing typed Source struct with Parse/String round-trippers and methods (CloneURL, TarballURL, CommitsAPIURL, OverlayDerivedSource, DisplayRef)

decisions.question: What is the architecture of the test infrastructure (tarballFakeServer, fault-injection, state-file factory)?
decisions.chosen: httptest.Server-based tarballFakeServer alongside localGitServer; single NIWA_TEST_FAULT env-var seam backed by a minimal internal/testfault/ package; Gherkin step pair (default + with-body docstring) backed by Go state-file factory

decisions.question: How is the GitHub tarball fetch client implemented?
decisions.chosen: Extend internal/github/client.go: HeadCommit + FetchTarball methods on APIClient; tar extraction in internal/github/tar.go; GH_TOKEN read once at constructor; redirects via CheckRedirect with chain inspection for rename detection; BaseURL substitution as the test seam

cross_validation_notes: No structural conflicts among the 5 chosen options. D1's inside-snapshot marker is swapped atomically with the rest of the snapshot under D2's two-rename. D2's sibling .niwa-state/ is independent of all other decisions. D3's leaf source package is consumed by but does not depend on D5 (D5 stays in github package). D4's tarballFakeServer integrates with D5 via APIClient's BaseURL constructor parameter. Two integration details deferred to Phase 4 architecture: (a) the production-code-side construction of APIClient — a NIWA_GITHUB_API_URL env var defaulting to https://api.github.com gives tests a clean override seam; (b) where exactly testfault.Maybe() calls land inside the github fetch + tar extraction path. Terminology nit: D5's 'GitHubFetcher' references the workspace-level snapshot writer that composes APIClient methods + tar extraction, not a separate type defined by D3 (D3's Source has no I/O methods).
